# Phase 2.1 DOC System Frontend Dev Agent

This file is the human-facing entry brief for the workspace's specialist DOC-system frontend development agent.

## Purpose

Use this agent when the authored DOC-system plan needs a stronger frontend-specialist implementation pass than the standard Phase 2 frontend dev agent.

It keeps the same DOC-system root, starter bootstrap, preservation-matrix discipline, and phase5-7 continuation contract, but adds higher-end creative frontend execution: visual direction, layout composition, interaction design, motion craft, app-like mobile UX, and production-grade frontend engineering.

## What It Owns

- specialist implementation of the authored DOC-system frontend plan
- creative but controlled visual-language and layout execution inside the preservation matrix
- shared surface redesign for adapted and replaced surfaces
- route-specific section storytelling, motion posture, and app-like mobile refinement
- maintainable Next.js frontend architecture and validation

## What It Does Not Own

- DOC-system planning itself
- backend or deploy lane changes
- phase3-only presentation polish after the main specialist implementation pass
- repo-topology, push-safety, or system-governance work

## Actual Invocable Agent

- Public wrapper: `.github/agents/phase2.1-doc-system-frontend-dev.agent.md`

## Supporting Files

- Shared baseline agent: `.github/agents/phase2-doc-system-frontend-dev.agent.md`
- Planning dependency: `.github/agents/phase2-doc-system-planning.agent.md`
- Specialist spec: `.github/doc-system-phase2-1-frontend-dev/phase2.1-doc-system-frontend-dev-spec.md`
- Readiness checklist: `.github/doc-system-phase2-1-frontend-dev/phase2.1-doc-system-frontend-dev-readiness-checklist.md`

## Rule Of Thumb

Use the standard Phase 2 DOC-system frontend dev agent when the authored transformation is mostly structural and deterministic.

Use this Phase 2.1 specialist agent when the plan needs stronger frontend-director execution quality: more intentional layout, more distinctive visual identity, richer interaction and motion, and genuinely app-like mobile behavior without breaking the DOC-system contract.
